summary: PR 2416 decompiled grkongo fn_801D8134 as a Kongo Jungle gorilla activation callback and moved the grgreens fn_802159B4 empty stub to the correct source position. The PR body claims fn_801D8134 is a 79-instruction 100% match; no review comments or independent verifier logs are present in the slice.

key_files.role: Replaced the fn_801D8134 decomp placeholder with C source for the Kongo Jungle gorilla activation callback and added needed efSync/ftLib includes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grkongo.c include additions for ef/efsync.h and ft/ftlib.h
raw_diff: /// #fn_801D8134 replaced by static int fn_801D8134(HSD_GObj* arg0, HSD_GObj* arg1)
human_pr_text: fn_801D8134 described as Kongo Jungle gorilla activation callback, 79 instructions, 100% match

key_files.role: Moved the empty fn_802159B4(Item_GObj*, Ground*) stub from an earlier location to the address-aligned placeholder location before grGreens_802159B8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: earlier fn_802159B4 empty stub removed near grGreens_80213C10
raw_diff: /// #fn_802159B4 replaced with empty return stub near grGreens_80215358/grGreens_802159B8
human_pr_text: fn_802159B4 reposition stub to correct address offset in grgreens.c

what_changed.text: fn_801D8134 in grkongo.c was implemented as a static int callback that returns 0 when the gorilla activation flag is already set or when the fighter is outside a squared-distance range check, and returns 1 after setting activation state and triggering related material/effect/fighter calls.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801D8134 body checks gp->gv.kongo3.xC6, calls Ground_801C4DA0 and ftLib_80086644, performs squared distance comparison against grKg_804D6980->unk28
raw_diff: fn_801D8134 sets gp->gv.kongo3.xCA, xD0, xC6 and calls Ground_801C5440, grMaterial_801C9604, efSync_Spawn, ftLib_80086C18
human_pr_text: checks if a fighter is within range of the gorilla spawn point, then activates the gorilla with a randomized delay

what_changed.text: grkongo.c gained includes for ef/efsync.h and ft/ftlib.h to support efSync_Spawn and ftLib calls used by the new function body.
fn_802159B4 in grgreens.c remained an empty return stub but was moved from an earlier source location to replace its decomp placeholder at the correct address offset.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removal of void fn_802159B4(Item_GObj* item_gobj, Ground* gp) { return; } near line 381
raw_diff: replacement of /// #fn_802159B4 with the same empty stub near line 580
human_pr_text: reposition stub to correct address offset in grgreens.c

smart_moves.text: The implementation used existing typed stage/fighter helpers and structs rather than raw pointer arithmetic for the recovered behavior.
The range check was expressed as a squared-distance comparison, avoiding an unnecessary square-root style operation in source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: (pos_gnd.x - pos_ft.x)^2 + y^2 + z^2 < grKg_804D6980->unk28 * grKg_804D6980->unk28

smart_moves.text: The grgreens stub fix was minimal and address-position focused, moving the stub without adding unrelated behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_802159B4 body remains only return;
human_pr_text: reposition stub to correct address offset in grgreens.c

decomp_lessons.lesson: For stage callbacks that depend on proximity, a matchable source shape may use typed position helpers plus an explicit squared-distance expression against a squared radius field.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: squared distance comparison against grKg_804D6980->unk28 * grKg_804D6980->unk28
human_pr_text: fn_801D8134 checks if a fighter is within range of the gorilla spawn point

decomp_lessons.lesson: A decomp PR can include a no-op stub relocation when the function body is already trivial but the source placement must match the correct address order.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_802159B4 empty stub removed from earlier location and inserted at placeholder
human_pr_text: reposition stub to correct address offset in grgreens.c

naming_conventions.name: pos_gnd
naming_conventions.convention: Ground position Vec3 local uses a short role suffix identifying the source of the position.

naming_conventions.name: pos_ft
naming_conventions.convention: Fighter position Vec3 local uses a short role suffix identifying the source of the position.

naming_conventions.name: rand_val
naming_conventions.convention: Random float result was named by its local role rather than kept as a temp_rXX-style name.

naming_conventions.name: diff
naming_conventions.convention: Difference between two config fields was named generically as diff; the slice does not prove a more semantic name for the grKg_804D6980 unk20/unk24 range.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used early exits via a shared done label for failure cases before the success path.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: failed range check goes to done

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Computed the randomized delay as a float range interpolation followed by an s16 cast into gp->gv.kongo3.xCA.
Added only the headers needed for newly referenced helpers instead of local declarations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: calls to efSync_Spawn, ftLib_80086644, and ftLib_80086C18

accepted_candidate_records.title: Stage proximity callbacks can match with typed helpers and squared-distance checks
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2416, grkongo fn_801D8134 used GET_GROUND, Ground_801C4DA0, ftLib_80086644, and a direct squared-distance expression against a squared config radius to implement a Kongo Jungle gorilla activation callback. Consider this as a searchable example for stage callback proximity checks, not as proof that all similar callbacks should use the same exact shape.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Kongo Jungle gorilla activation callback, 100% match claim
